decisions.title_en: Leaked audited OpenAI 2025 financials show $38.5B net loss on $13B revenue
decisions.reason: story_key not in lookback. Audited 2025 numbers (verified by FT) surfaced June 16: $13.07B revenue, $34B costs, $20.9B operating loss, $38.5B net loss inflated by ~$41.5B non-cash for-profit conversion charge.

decisions.title_en: G7 working lunch concludes: Amodei/Hassabis pitch US-led AI coalition; G7 adopts voluntary youth-safety commitments
decisions.reason: Published 06-12 (CEOs attending) and 06-15 (sovereignty splits). New on 06-17: the working lunch with Trump concluded; Amodei & Hassabis explicitly called for a US-led AI coalition with structured frontier-model access and China-excluding chip trade; G7 agreed a package of voluntary (non-binding) youth-safety commitments.

decisions.title_en: Microsoft routes GitHub through rival AWS as AI coding-agent load breaks enterprise SLAs
decisions.reason: story_key not in lookback. Microsoft confirmed June 16 it is routing GitHub traffic to AWS after AI agent commits (275M/week, on pace for 14B in 2026) pushed availability below the 99.9% enterprise SLA.

decisions.title_en: Google retires the Gemini CLI, forcing developers onto the new Antigravity CLI
decisions.reason: story_key not in lookback. June 18 is the hard cutoff retiring the legacy Gemini CLI for Pro/Ultra/free tiers; developers must migrate CI/CD and automation to Antigravity CLI (Gemini 3.5 Flash default).

decisions.title_en: Jensen Huang AP interview: society needs 'new social norms' for AI, urges everyone to use it
decisions.reason: story_key not in lookback. AP exclusive June 16: Huang says 'we need to create new social norms,' advocates universal AI use to close the tech divide, while conceding need for government regulation and safety standards.

decisions.title_en: Japan government and 3 megabanks granted access to Anthropic's restricted Claude Mythos
decisions.reason: Underlying access decision was reported by The Japan Times on 2026-06-03 (conveyed by Treasury's Bessent in Tokyo); >5 days old. June reblogs are rehashes, no concrete new dated development.

decisions.title_en: Anthropic leadership meets White House over Fable 5 export ban; dispute over jailbreak severity
decisions.reason: Already published 06-13 (export ban imposed) and 06-15 (Frontier Red Team flown to DC, Amazon warning revealed). June 16 meeting is incremental — no resolution, only a reported disagreement on jailbreak severity. Not substantive enough for a third day.

decisions.title_en: Google to pay SpaceX $920M/month (~$30B) for ~110k-GPU AI compute through 2029
decisions.reason: Deal signed June 5 (>5 days). Resurfaced June 16 as context in capacity-crisis coverage; folded as background into the github-aws-capacity-crisis item rather than published standalone.

decisions.title_en: SoftBank to invest up to €75B (€45B firm) in 5GW of French AI data centers
decisions.reason: Announced at Choose France summit on 2026-05-30/31; >5 days old. June 17 roundup mentions are rehashes.

decisions.title_en: SAP acquires Prior Labs, commits €1B+ to build a European frontier AI lab for structured data
Google ships Gemini 3.5 Flash, first Flash to beat the prior Pro tier
decisions.reason: Released at Google I/O on 2026-05-19; >5 days old.

decisions.title_en: Cursor's pre-acquisition $2B a16z round at $50B valuation
decisions.reason: The SpaceX $60B all-stock acquisition was already published 06-16. The earlier a16z funding round is superseded background, no new development.

decisions.title_en: Northwestern prints artificial neurons that communicate with living brain cells
decisions.reason: Published in Nature Nanotechnology April 15, 2026; >5 days old (recirculated in June science roundups).

decisions.title_en: DeepSeek releases open-source V4 model integrated with Huawei chips
Microsoft launches MAI-Thinking-1 and MAI-Code-1-Flash to reduce OpenAI reliance
Rhoda AI exits stealth with $450M Series A and FutureVision robotic platform
decisions.reason: Vague/early-June launch with no confirmed date within the 5-day window; insufficient corroboration.
